sourceMovie.title: The Astronaut
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Astronaut works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Astronaut clicked for you, 10 Cloverfield Lane (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Astronaut?
faq.answer: Try 10 Cloverfield Lane, A Quiet Place Part II, A Quiet Place: Day One—each shares DNA with The Astronaut while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Astronaut?
faq.answer: The Astronaut (2025) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Astronaut worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
